behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: Real failures commonly occur at the config-to-rule wiring boundary (merchant-category config lookup + scoring logic), which a pure unit test with injected constants can miss.
behaviors.test_description: Run scoring end-to-end across scoring modules using real config loading (or local config store) and a transaction whose amount exceeds its category threshold; assert the produced numeric score matches expected weighted rule output.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model judgment quality (pattern recognition/classification), not just deterministic wiring; mocked outputs would hide true model-performance failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute one real risk-model inference step on curated labeled transaction-context cases where rule thresholds stay below scoring limits but fraud pattern exists; assert fraud probability/classification crosses the configured fraud decision boundary for known-fraud examples.

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: Time-window counting is prone to boundary/query bugs across storage/time handling; in-process mocks can miss real filtering and timestamp semantics.
behaviors.test_description: With a local DB/transaction store, insert timestamped transactions around the window edges for one user, run velocity scoring, and assert counted transactions exactly match configured inclusive/exclusive window semantics.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: The key failure boundary is orchestration between model outputs and review-hold decision logic; isolated boolean unit tests can miss integration/wiring regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the decision pipeline with scoring output below hold threshold and risk output flagged=true from their real integration interfaces; assert transaction state transitions to manual-review hold.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: This is an output-contract composition problem across scoring, risk, and response-building modules; pure unit tests can miss dropped/renamed fields in integrated payload construction.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the combined evaluation path and validate the returned structured payload contains both fields (risk_score, fraud_probability) plus per-model flag attribution metadata consumed by review UI/API.
